fields.title: Chapter 3 - The Battle for Monte d’Or

fields.text: Timer Starts on Selecting OK on the Naming Screen
Tap on Floating Clown Stumble (Top Right)
Tap on Back to Map (Bottom Mid)
Tap on Back to Map (Bottom Mid)
Minigame - Pick a Random Cup twice
Minigame - Pay attention to his finger
Move to Ledore Mansion Garden (Top Right)
Move Back (Out of the Ledore Parlor)
Move Back (Out of the Ledore Mansion Garden)
Choose the rabbit - Give a name
Change to Yellow the D house
Swap the Top Right panel with the Middle one
Move Up until Randall talks to you
Tap on Dig Hole (Bottom Mid) on the Top Right dirt patch
Move Right until you find a Glowing Spot
Tap on Dig Hole on the Glowing Spot
Move Right to press the other button
Move Up until you find a rock
Move Up until you find two rock
Tap on Push looking at the left rock
Move Up until you find a rock
Move Up to the third level
Move Up to the glowing spots
Move to the stairs and press the button
Wait for the mummy to fall into the hole
Wait for the mummy to move along the top horizontal track
Wait for the mummy to fall into the hole
Follow the path to the right
Follow the path to the right
IMPORTANT: TO “TILT” YOUR 3DS ON EMULATOR, YOU NEED TO HOLD THE RIGHT MOUSE CLICK (M2) AND THEN YOU CAN MOVE IT AS YOU NEED
Tilt the 3DS up and move Layton as much as you can
Tap on Top Left Botton twice to face left
Tap on Top Left Botton twice to face down
Tap on Books (Table on the Right)
Tap on Drop down when you see the J letter
Tap on Drop down when you see the L letter
Time Ends on Pressing Okay on Mystery Screen

fields.fact: Hint coins are hidden all over the place. Use your magnifying glass in Investigation Mode to find them: when the magnifying glass turns orange, touch that spot. Collect hint coins to help you solve puzzles.
Sometimes, you may have to solve a certain puzzle in order to progress in the story.

As the story advances and you move to new locations, you might lose access to some puzzles. You can always visit the Puzzle Palace, the gathering place for all lost puzzles. Not all puzzles go to the Puzzle Palace — some hidden puzzles stay right where they are.

Picarats are units that tell you how difficult a puzzle is. When you solve a puzzle, you receive the number of picarats displayed on the introductory screen. Giving an incorrect answer means you will receive fewer picarats when you eventually solve the puzzle. Collect picarats to unlock extra content in the Top Secret section in Bonuses.
When you answer a puzzle incorrectly, you have the option to try it again straight away, view hints by spending hint coins, or quit the puzzle and leave it for later.

All the puzzles you've encountered are stored in the Puzzle Index. You can replay any puzzles you've solved as many times as you like, and view the hints for them without spending any hint coins. To try an unsolved puzzle again, look for it at the location indicated on the top screen.
Odd happenings and mysterious things that you hear during the course of the story are recorded under Mysteries in the Professor's Trunk, with a short summary for each.
Touch SAVE in the Professor's Trunk to save your progress. There are three files; any data already in a file will be overwritten if you choose to save to that file, so be careful.

In Movement Mode, touch an orange icon on the map to move to that location. Touch the magnifying glass icon to switch to Investigation Mode and examine your surroundings; an orange magnifying glass marks a spot of interest, a blue one marks a zoom point.
